scope_note: v0.4.0 declares this baseline as shared (skill stability is intrinsically about the SKILL, language-agnostic). Decision D2 (resolved 2026-04-27 post-R2): Lenient default — one shared score across languages. Progressive fallback: shared-core skills with generalization_debt (tdd-patterns, idempotent-retry-safety, network-error-classification) flip to per-language only if Phase B data shows ≥3pp divergence; D6=Split (rule shared, examples in <pack>/conventions.yaml) is the canonical resolution. See docs/LANGUAGE-AGNOSTIC-DECISIONS.md and docs/R2-DEBT-REWRITE-FEASIBILITY.md.
note: SDK-mode skill-health baseline per CLAUDE.md Rule #23 and baseline-manager SDK-MODE Delta 2. Tracks longitudinal skill stability under learning-engine body-patch regime (no runtime skill synthesis). First baseline established from sdk-dragonfly-s2 F4a/F4b/F7 signals. Run 2 appended from first Python pilot sdk-resourcepool-py-pilot-v1 (2026-04-29). Metrics raise-only per their natural direction; baseline kept on regression.

skill_stability.computation_note: 2 patch-level bumps applied (go-error-handling-patterns 1.0.0→1.0.1, go-example-function-patterns 0.1.0→0.1.1) across 19 skills invoked Run 1 = 2/19 = 0.105. Target <0.3 after 10-run rolling avg. Rolling avg over Run 1 + Run 2 (Python) = (0.105 + 0.0) / 2 = 0.053 — below target. Baseline kept at 0.105 (raise-only on stability metric direction).

history.note: 0 skill patches applied this run; learning-engine F6 not yet invoked at time of baseline-manager.

existing_skill_patch_accept_rate.computation_note: 2/2 F7 patch-level skill bumps applied without rollback (SP-01 error-handling triggers, SP-02 example-function triggers). No golden-corpus gate executed (golden-corpus/dragonfly-v1/ empty; human-seed pending per seq 151). Accept rate is nominal 1.0 but caveat: corpus-gate bypassed.

history.note: No patches issued by learning-engine F6 at the time baseline-manager ran; metric not measurable this run. Inherits prior baseline.

manifest_miss_rate.computation_note: Run 1 (Go): G24 §Guardrails-Manifest PASS (0/38 BLOCKER misses). G23 §Skills-Manifest WARN (8/27 misses = 0.296). Run 2 (Python): G24 PASS (0/19), G23 PASS (0/22). Both rates IMPROVED to 0.0. Rolling: blocking still 0.0; warn improved Run 1→Run 2 from 0.296→0.0. Target-direction: down. Baseline updated to Run 2 values (improvement permits raise of the 'better' baseline).

learning_patches_reverted_by_user.computation_note: Replaces the retired golden_regression_rate metric (full-replay regression retired post-sdk-dragonfly-s2 — dominated Phase 4 token spend with no signal beyond the devil fleet). Counts how many learning-engine patches the user reverted at H10 review of feedback/learning-notifications.md. Target: trending down (well-calibrated notifications). Run 2 H10 not yet reached; pending.

history.note: H10 not yet reached at baseline-manager F7 time

mean_time_to_green_sec.computation_note: Not measured this run (no wall-clock instrumentation for first-green-test). Run 2 also not instrumented. Monitor trend once metrics-collector F1 adds this signal.

user_overrides: Mode A override at run-start (TPRD §16 declared Mode B; user directed greenfield regeneration)
DEP-BUMP-UNAPPROVED Option A resolution at seq 38/39 (testcontainers-go MVS bumps approved)
user_intervention_rate.computation_note: Counting 'overrides' = user directives that deviated from declared pipeline path; not all HITL approvals count as interventions. Run 1 baseline = 2 overrides. Target trending down; flat or up signals gate miscalibration. Run 2 (Python) overrides: 1 (H7 option-1 accept-INCOMPLETE-on-G43-py); H8-calibration-warn was a documented disposition not an override. Net: 1 override (down from 2 — improvement direction). Baseline updated: minimum trend.

history.note: H7 option-1 INCOMPLETE-G43-py acceptance was the only directive override; H8 PA-013 calibration-warn and H9 3-disposition acceptance were standard HITL gate-pass approvals.

raise_policy.description: Skill-health is a mixed-direction dashboard: stability/accept-rate/green-time target improvement; miss-rate/intervention-rate/learning_patches_reverted_by_user target decrease. Baselines are locked at current values on first run; future runs are evaluated per-metric in their natural direction. Never lower a 'better' baseline (lower-is-better metrics keep their minimum; higher-is-better metrics keep their maximum) without learning-engine reset authorization.
